heading: Options to buy or sell
label: 1234 Options to buy or sell

children.heading: Treatment of gain or loss in the case of the purchaser
children.label: a Treatment of gain or loss in the case of the purchaser

children.heading: General rule
children.label: 1 General rule
children.text: Gain or loss attributable to the sale or exchange of, or loss attributable to failure to exercise, an option to buy or sell property shall be considered gain or loss from the sale or exchange of property which has the same character as the property to which the option relates has in the hands of the taxpayer (or would have in the hands of the taxpayer if acquired by him).

children.heading: Special rule for loss attributable to failure to exercise option
children.label: 2 Special rule for loss attributable to failure to exercise option
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1), if loss is attributable to failure to exercise an option, the option shall be deemed to have been sold or exchanged on the day it expired.

children.label: A
children.text: an option which constitutes property described in paragraph (1) of section 1221(a);

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of gain attributable to the sale or exchange of an option, any income derived in connection with such option which, without regard to this subsection, is treated as other than gain from the sale or exchange of a capital asset; and

children.label: C
children.text: a loss attributable to failure to exercise an option described in section 1233(c).

children.heading: Treatment of grantor of option in the case of stock, securities, or commodities
children.label: b Treatment of grantor of option in the case of stock, securities, or commodities

children.heading: General rule
children.label: 1 General rule
children.text: In the case of the grantor of the option, gain or loss from any closing transaction with respect to, and gain on lapse of, an option in property shall be treated as a gain or loss from the sale or exchange of a capital asset held not more than 1 year.

children.heading: Closing transaction
children.label: A Closing transaction
children.text: The term “closing transaction” means any termination of the taxpayer’s obligation under an option in property other than through the exercise or lapse of the option.

children.heading: Property
children.label: B Property
children.text: The term “property” means stocks and securities (including stocks and securities dealt with on a “when issued” basis), commodities, and commodity futures.

children.heading: Nonapplication of subsection
children.label: 3 Nonapplication of subsection
children.text: This subsection shall not apply to any option granted in the ordinary course of the taxpayer’s trade or business of granting options.

children.heading: Treatment of options on section 1256 contracts and cash settlement options
children.label: c Treatment of options on section 1256 contracts and cash settlement options

children.heading: Section 1256 contracts
children.label: 1 Section 1256 contracts
children.text: Gain or loss shall be recognized on the exercise of an option on a section 1256 contract (within the meaning of section 1256(b)).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For purposes of subsections (a) and (b), a cash settlement option shall be treated as an option to buy or sell property.

children.heading: Cash settlement option
children.label: B Cash settlement option
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), the term “cash settlement option” means any option which on exercise settles in (or could be settled in) cash or property other than the underlying property.
